meta_title: For Students: project management to Solve slow development speed (Intermediate Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to project management and overcome slow development speed. A Intermediate-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Project Management for Students: Stop Slow Dev Cycles
content.seo_description: Overcome slow development speed with this intermediate guide. Master project management using Antigravity and Claude Code to launch your first SaaS app.
content.answer_block: To eliminate slow development speed, shift from chaotic coding to structured project management. By integrating Antigravity for architectural planning and Claude Code for rapid implementation, you transform your workflow from reactive debugging into a streamlined assembly line, allowing you to ship production-grade features in hours rather than weeks.
content.intro:
The cursor blinks in the void, mocking the mountain of code you still need to write. You started with passion, but now you’re drowning in technical debt, scope creep, and the crushing realization that your 'simple' feature has turned into a three-week slog. The dream of shipping your first SaaS feels like it's slipping away behind a wall of unorganized tasks and fragmented logic.

But here is the industry secret: elite engineers don't just code faster; they manage complexity before they even type a function. By treating your development lifecycle as a productized system rather than a series of chores, you reclaim your momentum. This case study shows you how to leverage AI-driven project management to turn your development speed from a bottleneck into your greatest competitive advantage.

steps.title: Define the Atomic Scope
steps.description: Break your massive feature into 'atomic' tasks that take less than 4 hours to complete. This prevents scope creep.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my project requirements and break them down into an ordered list of atomic tasks suitable for a 2-week sprint. Focus on MVPs only.
steps.claude_code_command: claude task --create 'Break down feature X into sub-tasks with clear acceptance criteria'

steps.title: Architect the Data Schema
steps.description: Don't build in the dark. Map your database schema before writing a single line of application logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a Prisma schema for a SaaS user dashboard including subscriptions, project tracking, and analytics storage.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate schema.prisma file and execute migrations'

steps.title: Implement Stubbed Infrastructure
steps.description: Build the skeleton of your app (API routes, auth, database connections) before filling in the business logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write the boilerplate for a Next.js API route handler that includes standard error handling and Zod validation.

steps.title: Automate Unit Test Generation
steps.description: Use AI to write tests for your core logic as you build, ensuring you don't break code as you scale.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create Jest test suites for the core authentication functions defined in my services directory.
steps.claude_code_command: claude test --watch 'Write unit tests for authService.ts'

steps.title: Continuous Integration Sync
steps.description: Sync your AI-managed tasks with your GitHub repository to maintain visibility on your progress.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a GitHub Actions workflow that runs my test suite on every pull request.

pro_tips: Never write code that isn't tied to a specific task ID in your project board.
Use Claude Code to perform 'self-code reviews' before committing to main to catch logic errors early.
If a task takes longer than 4 hours, it is too big; decompose it further until it feels trivial.
Maintain a 'Decision Log' markdown file to track why you chose specific libraries or architectures.
Leverage the 'Context' feature in your IDE to keep AI updated on your entire codebase, not just the current file.
content.next_steps: Once your first feature is live, implement a feedback loop by deploying to Vercel and monitoring errors with Sentry. Refine your workflow based on where you spent the most time debugging.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools and the strategy. Now you need the execution. Stop guessing and start building with a framework that scales with your ambition. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
